2604.09907.title: From UAV Imagery to Agronomic Reasoning: A Multimodal LLM Benchmark for Plant Phenotyping
2604.09907.paper_summary: To improve crop genetics, high-throughput, effective and comprehensive phenotyping is a critical prerequisite. While such tasks were traditionally performed manually, recent advances in multimodal foundation models, especially in vision-language models (VLMs), have enabled more automated and robust phenotypic analysis. However, plant science remains a particularly challenging domain for foundation models because it requires domain-specific knowledge, fine-grained visual interpretation, and complex biological and agronomic reasoning. To address this gap, we develop PlantXpert, an evidence-grounded multimodal reasoning benchmark for soybean and cotton phenotyping. Our benchmark provides a structured and reproducible framework for agronomic adaptation of VLMs, and enables controlled comparison between base models and their domain-adapted counterparts. We constructed a dataset comprising 385 digital images and more than 3,000 benchmark samples spanning key plant science domains including disease, pest control, weed management, and yield. The benchmark can assess diverse capabilities including visual expertise, quantitative reasoning, and multi-step agronomic reasoning. A total of 11 state-of-the-art VLMs were evaluated. The results indicate that task-specific fine-tuning leads to substantial improvement in accuracy, with models such as Qwen3-VL-4B and Qwen3-VL-30B achieving up to 78%. At the same time, gains from model scaling diminish beyond a certain capacity, generalization across soybean and cotton remains uneven, and quantitative as well as biologically grounded reasoning continue to pose substantial challenges. These findings suggest that PlantXpert can serve as a foundation for assessing evidence-grounded agronomic reasoning and for advancing multimodal model development in plant science.
2604.09907.paper_summary_zh: 為了改善作物基因，進行高通量、有效且全面的表型分析是關鍵的前提條件。雖然這些任務傳統上是手動執行的，但最近在多模態基礎模型方面的進展，特別是在視覺-語言模型（VLMs）中，使得更自動化和穩健的表型分析成為可能。然而，植物科學對於基礎模型來說仍然是一個特別具有挑戰性的領域，因為它需要特定領域的知識、細緻的視覺解釋以及複雜的生物和農業推理。為了解決這一差距，我們開發了PlantXpert，一個以證據為基礎的多模態推理基準，專注於大豆和棉花的表型分析。我們的基準提供了一個結構化和可重複的框架，用於VLM的農業適應，並使基礎模型與其領域適應版本之間的控制比較成為可能。我們構建了一個數據集，包含385張數字圖像和超過3,000個基準樣本，涵蓋了包括疾病、害蟲控制、雜草管理和產量等關鍵植物科學領域。該基準可以評估多種能力，包括視覺專業知識、定量推理和多步驟農業推理。共評估了11個最先進的VLM。結果顯示，針對特定任務的微調會顯著提高準確性，像Qwen3-VL-4B和Qwen3-VL-30B等模型的準確率高達78%。同時，模型擴展所帶來的增益在達到某一容量後會減少，對於大豆和棉花的泛化仍然不均勻，定量以及生物學基礎的推理仍然面臨重大挑戰。這些發現表明，PlantXpert可以作為評估以證據為基礎的農業推理的基礎，並推進植物科學中多模態模型的發展。
2604.09907.author: Yu Wu et.al.
2604.09907.authors: Yu Wu, Guangzeng Han, Ibra Niang Niang, Francia Ravelombola, Maiara Oliveira, Jason Davis, Dong Chen, Feng Lin, Xiaolei Huang
